title: Body Parsing, Raw Webhooks, Forms, Multipart Uploads, and Byte Limits · Inference Brief: Raw Webhook Byte Ownership
summary: Parsers accept decompression bombs, req.body is assumed present, raw webhook bytes are lost, multipart data is buffered without caps, and aborted uploads leak work. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts raw webhook byte ownership increment in bounded body ownership and parser matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Preserve exact raw bytes for signature verification while separating timestamp, replay, media type, decode, validation, and enqueue decisions.
Enforce declared, compressed, decoded, field-count, nesting, and semantic-size budgets before expensive parsing or effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
